# Investment Memo — Northwind Logistics (NWL), Initiation

## Summary

We initiate coverage of Northwind Logistics with an **Overweight** rating and a 12-month target of
$58 (vs. $47 spot), ~23% upside. The thesis rests on margin recovery as the fleet-renewal capex cycle
rolls off, supported by management guidance[^nwl-q4] and freight-rate normalization[^cass-2025].

## Thesis

NWL is a regional less-than-truckload carrier whose operating margin compressed during a two-year
fleet replacement. We expect operating margin to recover from 9.1%[^nwl-q4] toward the 12% pre-cycle
level as depreciation normalizes. Key assumptions, all carried into the model below:

- Revenue grows 6% in FY26 on volume, not price, consistent with the freight cycle[^cass-2025].
- Capex falls from 11% to 7% of revenue as the renewal program completes[^nwl-q4].
- No equity issuance; net debt reduced from free cash flow.

## Valuation

We use a sum-of-the-parts on FY27 estimates. Component values (in $M):

<!-- reconcile -->
- Core LTL operating value: 8,200
- Logistics-tech segment: 1,400
- Net cash and equivalents: 600
- Total enterprise-to-equity value: 10,200
<!-- /reconcile -->

On 176M shares this implies ~$58/share. The core segment is valued at 7.0x EV/EBITDA, a discount to
the peer median of 8.2x[^peer-comp], justified by NWL's smaller scale.

## Risks

- **Freight recession:** a demand downturn would stall the volume assumption[^cass-2025].
- **Execution:** the fleet program could overrun, keeping capex elevated[^nwl-q4].
- **Customer concentration:** the top five shippers are ~30% of revenue[^nwl-q4].

## Disclosures

The analyst holds no position in NWL. This note is for the firm's discretionary mandate and is not
investment advice to retail clients. Figures are sourced from public filings and the vendors cited.

## Sources

[^nwl-q4]: Northwind Logistics FY2025 Q4 10-K, filed 2026-02-18.
[^cass-2025]: Cass Freight Index, March 2026 release.
[^peer-comp]: Internal peer comparables sheet, LTL carriers, 2026-03-01.
